title: Automation - Send email to defined teams
excerpt:

I’m quite new to monday and right now I have one question.

posts.author: thludwig (Thorsten Ludwig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everybody.

I’m quite new to monday and right now I have one question.

I have defined teams, which I can see in my profile (see attached pic).
Is it possible to send an email to one or more of these teams within an automation?

When I select team I only get a blank window and can’t see any teams or names.

Regards
Thorsten

monday_select teams for automated email -- blank1127×562 35.4 KB

monday_teams in profile901×835 43.9 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’ll need to invite the team you want to notify to the board. Click on invite at top right and then search for the team. Refresh, wait a minute and then try the automation again.

image1182×183 12.6 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: thludwig (Thorsten Ludwig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Desiree.

Thanks a lot, that helped. I had already invited all the people from the teams but I didn’t know that I have to invite the teams as well.

Now I have another problem. I have 6 teams that will be informed when items change. Since I didn’t want to get 6 emails each time I make a change I removed me from 2 of the teams…until I realized, I am COMPLETELEY removed from them, so I even can’t manage them. How silly is that? I am the creator of the teams and unless I hand it over to one of the members I should always have access to them, no matter if I’m a member of it.

Sheesh…I’m considering returning to the people columns and add the teams there.

But thanks for your reply!

Thorsten

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad I could help! And yes, being able to manage a team without being in it, would be helpful. Maybe put it on the feature suggestion board? Feature requests - monday Community Forum.

posts.author: thludwig (Thorsten Ludwig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Almost forgot an important part of my question:
Is it possible to send an email to more than one of these teams within an automation?

Thorsten

posts.author: thludwig (Thorsten Ludwig)
posts.cookedHtml: nevermind, I found out I can add more actions and email to other teams. Would be nice if I could add more than one team in one (email) action
